skill.name: research-fact-checker
skill.description: Assists authors in researching topics, verifying facts, and ensuring accuracy in their writing. Particularly useful for historical fiction, non-fiction, science fiction, and any genre requiring real-world accuracy. Use when the user needs help with research or fact-checking.
skill.summary: Helps authors plan research, verify manuscript claims, and integrate accurate details into narrative writing.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, weak-crypto keywords, and reconnaissance-like text, but review found they are documentation examples and prose only. The skill contains research workflows, report templates, and writing examples, with no executable code, network instructions, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection text found.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Alerts Are Markdown Templates
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The backtick findings are Markdown code fences around report templates and sample outputs, not Ruby or shell command execution. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The flagged locations open or close fenced Markdown examples, and no command language or execution instruction appears in those blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is direct: each location is a Markdown fence in documentation, not a shell or Ruby execution context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Word Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-cryptography alerts occur in normal writing and sample content, such as descriptions, historical examples, and prose about M1 Garand rifles. No cryptographic algorithm selection, hashing, credential handling, or encryption workflow is present. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The surrounding text is narrative research guidance, and no security-sensitive cryptographic operation exists in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain prose and checklist content, with no code path that could use weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alert Is Narrative Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The system reconnaissance alert is caused by a quoted narrative example about a rifle making a ping sound. It does not instruct the model to run ping, scan systems, or gather host information. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The term appears inside a fictional prose example and has no operational command context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding paragraph is a writing example, so the scanner matched a benign English word.

content.user_title: Verify Research and Facts for Manuscripts
content.value_statement: Authors need accurate details without slowing the writing process. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured methods for research planning, claim checking, and source tracking.
actual_capabilities: Creates research briefs with questions, context, source types, and verification checklists.
Extracts factual claims from manuscript passages and organizes them for review.
Flags inaccuracies, anachronisms, unverifiable claims, and weak source support.
Suggests more accurate revisions that preserve narrative flow.
Builds bibliography templates for sources, experts, research notes, and follow-up topics.
limitations: It does not perform live web browsing unless the host AI tool provides browsing.
It cannot guarantee factual accuracy without reliable external sources.
It is not a substitute for qualified legal, medical, cultural, or scientific experts.
It focuses on writing research and does not run automated verification tools.

use_cases.title: Historical Fiction Detail Review
use_cases.description: Check settings, clothing, language, objects, and social norms for period accuracy before revision.

use_cases.title: Non-Fiction Claim Verification
use_cases.description: Turn chapter claims into a checklist with source needs, confidence notes, and correction options.

use_cases.title: Science Fiction Plausibility Pass
use_cases.description: Review scientific details and choose where to preserve accuracy or document deliberate invention.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Research Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a research brief for my story topic: [topic]. Include key questions, source types, red flags, and a verification checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Passage
prompt_templates.prompt: Fact-check this passage for accuracy, anachronisms, and unsupported claims. Keep corrections practical for revision: [paste passage].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Source Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a source plan for [subject]. Separate primary sources, secondary sources, expert input, and facts that need cross-checking.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Accuracy Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this chapter for factual risk. Categorize claims by severity, explain likely reader impact, and suggest revisions that keep the scene natural: [paste chapter].

output_examples.input: I need to research an American art student in 1920s Paris.
output_examples.output: A research brief with daily life topics, art school details, expatriate culture, source suggestions, red flags, and story integration notes.

output_examples.input: Fact-check a Mars colony scene where characters breathe outside without suits.
output_examples.output: A claim-by-claim accuracy report that flags atmosphere, pressure, temperature, radiation, and sky visibility issues with revision options.

output_examples.input: Help me track sources for a historical novel.
output_examples.output: A bibliography structure with source titles, authors, dates, reliability notes, chapter usage, expert consultations, and follow-up research tasks.

best_practices: Provide the genre, time period, location, and target accuracy level before asking for review.
Ask for source quality notes when facts will affect plot, safety, culture, or credibility.
Use the revision suggestions as drafting support, then verify important facts with independent sources.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated facts as final citations without checking reliable sources.
Do not ask for a full manuscript audit without giving priority areas or accuracy standards.
Do not use the skill to replace expert review for high-stakes or culturally sensitive material.

faq.question: Does this skill browse the web?
faq.answer: No. It provides research and fact-checking workflows. Live browsing depends on the AI tool environment.

faq.question: Can it cite sources?
faq.answer: It can organize citation needs and source notes, but users should verify final citations against real sources.

faq.question: Is it useful for fiction?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for historical fiction, science fiction, fantasy, and other detail-sensitive genres.

faq.question: Can it review non-fiction chapters?
faq.answer: Yes. It can extract claims, rate verification needs, and suggest corrections for unsupported statements.

faq.question: Does it replace sensitivity readers?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend cultural research steps, but human experts are needed for sensitive representation.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the passage, setting, intended genre, target audience, and how strictly the work must follow real facts.
